title: Typed Bucket Endpoint and S3 Express Compatibility · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds bucket and endpoint compatibility matrix, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bucket and endpoint compatibility matrix while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, construct regional virtual-hosted and zonal endpoints without confusing region, availability zone, bucket name, TLS name, or path style.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, resolve intended region and endpoint explicitly and reject redirects, signing-region drift, and accidental cross-region transfer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop General Directory Bucket
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8691 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow bucket type before validating directory-bucket endpoint and session state; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then change region, retrieval, or transfer cost and defend the revised budget action. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop General Directory Bucket.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop General Directory Bucket, keep General Directory Bucket connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop General Directory Bucket, name the stakeholder, unknown schema, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, promise, stream, cache, cost, abort, cleanup, and recovery boundary, General Directory Bucket precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop General Directory Bucket, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 5123 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to narrow bucket type before validating directory-bucket endpoint and session state; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop General Directory Bucket, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Directory buckets are faster general-purpose buckets with identical APIs and guarantees. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Bucket Naming Creation Gate
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-bucket-naming-creation-gate-1vbn8f" that uses case 2532 to narrow bucket type before validating directory-bucket endpoint and session state. Guided rehearsal: predict the branch, make one bounded increment, and inspect peer-readable changed output. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Bucket Naming Creation Gate.
